Identity.History: Skandagupta ruled the Gupta Empire around 455–467 AD and was the last of its great imperial monarchs. Son of Kumaragupta I, he came to power amid crisis, defeating internal challengers and repelling major invasions, especially by the Huns or related Central Asian groups. His inscriptions present him as a restorer of Gupta power, and his reign saw repairs to public works such as the Sudarshana Lake dam. Though victorious, the wars strained Gupta finances and administration. After his death, the empire gradually weakened and fragmented.
